description: A polished Department of Labor 'good news roundup' graphic on a dark navy background with a faint watermarked DOL seal. The headline at top reads in large white and gold all-caps text 'THE TRUMP ECONOMY IS STRONG!' (with 'IS STRONG!' in gold), underlined by a row of three gold stars. Below sits a 2x2 grid of mock news-outlet headline cards, each tagged with a recognizable media logo on a black banner: Bloomberg ('US Core Capital Goods Orders Jump by Most Since 2020'), Zillow ('Renters gain more than $2,300 in breathing room as rent growth hits slowest pace since 2020'), AP ('Weekly US jobless claims fall to 189,000, lowest in more than 5 decades'), and MarketWatch ('Construction on new homes hits 15-month high'). The whole grid is framed by a thin gold border. At the bottom is the official U.S. Department of Labor seal and wordmark with a globe icon and 'dol.gov'. The propaganda technique is borrowed credibility / 'third-party endorsement': curating favorable mainstream-press headlines into one branded card to imply objective media validation of Trump-era economic success. Target audience: general public, workers, and economic-messaging followers.
notable_text: THE TRUMP ECONOMY IS STRONG! / US Core Capital Goods Orders Jump by Most Since 2020 / Renters gain more than $2,300 in breathing room / Weekly US jobless claims fall to 189,000, lowest in more than 5 decades / Construction on new homes hits 15-month high / U.S. DEPARTMENT OF LABOR / dol.gov
